result:
The experiment is pre-registered in `runs/pending_experiment.json` and the figure passes figcheck. Summary of what I designed and why:

**Where the history stands.** The plateau rule was in force: exps 26, 27, 28 were all reverted, all in the trunk-capacity-scaling family. The joint record now excludes the previously suspected bottlenecks — exp 20 removed memorization (train and eval medians are equal), exp 25 trained to a genuine loss plateau for only a 5.2% gain (exonerating optimization), and exps 26/28 showed 3× trunk depth/channels doesn't help at the designed operating point. Exp 26's own pre-registered fork named what's left: the information limit of the readout/head.

**Exp 29: stride-8 fine-layout tap.** The field scorer today sees only a 560-d summary of the crop — a 48-d GAP plus a 512-d layout code squeezed from the stride-16 8×8 grid — so nothing finer than 16 m granularity ever reaches the 1024-way cell scorer, while the ~215 m map cells it must tell apart differ precisely in sub-16-m arrangement (building outlines, road widths). Two signatures point at this: midday errors equal night errors (clean daytime detail goes unused), and self-similar metros do worse than distinctive rural Prignitz (lookalike confusion, not lighting). The change taps the trunk's stride-8 activation (block 3, 16×16×24), squeezes it with a 1×1 conv to a 1,024-d fine layout code, and concatenates it into the bright field head (input 560 → 1,584). The trunk, C4 vote, decode, confidence, and training procedure stay byte-identical — unlike the refuted experiments, no capacity is added to the encoder; the head is just allowed to see a scale the encoder already computes and discards. The enlarged FC heads ship int8 via the probe-proven `quantize_dynamic` MatMul/Gemm recipe (~7 m measured shift), landing the export at ~2.6 MB against the 4 MiB gate, with an fp32 debug ONNX saved for forensics.

**Prediction.** Kept iff worst-case median < 797.91 m; predicted 650–750 m, gains largest in daytime buckets and metros. Refutation forks are pre-registered: a flat result exonerates readout width and points at rotation residual / intrinsic footprint ambiguity next.

I also saved a memory noting that the scorer's median-over-kept metric couples with coverage — exp 28's 1057 m was measured at higher coverage than the 798 m best, so the "capacity refuted" record is partially confounded; future comparisons should check per-cell coverage in both metrics.json files before drawing conclusions.

tool_input.command:
.venv/bin/python -c "
import json, cairosvg
svg = json.load(open('runs/pending_experiment.json'))['architecture_svg']
cairosvg.svg2png(bytestring=svg.encode(), write_to='/tmp/arch_iter9.png', output_width=1470)
print('ok')
" 2>&1 | tail -1 || (command -v rsvg-convert && .venv/bin/python -c "
import json; open('/tmp/a.svg','w').write(json.load(open('runs/pending_experiment.json'))['architecture_svg'])" && rsvg-convert -w 1470 /tmp/a.svg -o /tmp/arch_iter9.png && echo ok)
command -v rsvg-convert inkscape convert chromium chromium-browser google-chrome 2>/dev/null; ls /usr/bin | grep -i -E "svg|magick" | head
command -v rsvg-convert inkscape convert chromium google-chrome npx

.venv/bin/pip install cairosvg -q 2>&1 | tail -1; .venv/bin/python -c "
import json, cairosvg
svg = json.load(open('runs/pending_experiment.json'))['architecture_svg']
cairosvg.svg2png(bytestring=svg.encode(), write_to='/tmp/arch_iter9.png', output_width=1960, background_color='white')
print('ok')
"
tool_input.description: Install cairosvg and render figure to PNG
